title: How America First Risks Becoming America Alone
title_cn: “美国优先”如何面临“美国孤立”的风险

paragraphs.cn: 美国东部时间2026年1月30日晚9:00

paragraphs.en: BERLIN—Few cities are more emblematic of the postwar trans-Atlantic alliance than the German capital. In 1963, John F. Kennedy delivered his famous “Ich bin ein Berliner” speech here promising American solidarity during the Soviet blockade. In 1987, Ronald Reagan stood before the Berlin Wall and challenged Soviet leader Mikhail Gorbachev to tear it down. And 21 years later, 200,000 Berliners turned out to hear then-Sen. Barack Obama herald a new era of U.S.-European friendship.
paragraphs.cn: 柏林——很少有城市比德国首都更能象征战后跨大西洋联盟。1963年，约翰·F·肯尼迪在这里发表了他著名的“我是一个柏林人”演讲，承诺在苏联封锁期间美国将与柏林团结一致。1987年，罗纳德·里根站在柏林墙前，挑战苏联领导人米哈伊尔·戈尔巴乔夫将其拆除。21年后，20万柏林人涌上街头，聆听时任参议员巴拉克·奥巴马预示美欧友谊新时代的到来。

paragraphs.en: Today, the mood toward Uncle Sam in Berlin, and much of the world, has darkened. After the tumultuous first year of Donald Trump’s second term, few Germans have positive things to say about the current U.S. president, partly because he doesn’t have nice things to say either. During a speech in neighboring Switzerland last week, Trump taunted Europeans that without U.S. help in World War II, “you’d all be speaking German.”
paragraphs.cn: 如今，柏林乃至世界大部分地区对美国的态度已经变得阴沉。在唐纳德·特朗普第二任期动荡的第一年之后，很少有德国人对现任美国总统有好话，部分原因是他自己也没有好话可说。上周在邻国瑞士的一次演讲中，特朗普嘲讽欧洲人，称如果没有美国在二战中的帮助，“你们都会说德语”。

paragraphs.en: “My grandfather was from Hawaii, he came as a GI, so I always had a very trusted connection to the U.S.,” said Natascha Ahrens, a 59-year-old office manager. “ I find the image of the U.S. that Trump is spreading around the world to be absolutely devastating.”
paragraphs.cn: 59岁的办公室经理娜塔莎·阿伦斯说：“我的祖父来自夏威夷，他是一名美国大兵，所以我一直与美国有着非常信任的联系。我认为特朗普在世界各地散布的美国形象绝对是毁灭性的。”

paragraphs.en: Welcome to a world where America First is coming to mean America Alone, whose friends are searching for alternatives to what increasingly feels like an abusive relationship and whose enemies are gloating.
paragraphs.cn: 欢迎来到一个“美国优先”正逐渐演变为“美国孤立”的世界，在这个世界里，美国的朋友们正在寻找替代方案，以摆脱这种日益感觉像虐待的关系，而美国的敌人则幸灾乐祸。

paragraphs.en:

paragraphs.cn: 订阅《华尔街日报》以继续阅读您的文章

vocabulary.meaning_en: Serving as a symbol of a particular quality or concept; symbolic.
vocabulary.example: Few cities are more emblematic of the postwar trans-Atlantic alliance than the German capital.
vocabulary.example_cn: 很少有城市比德国首都更能象征战后跨大西洋联盟。

vocabulary.meaning_en: A political, military, or economic alliance involving countries on both sides of the Atlantic Ocean, typically referring to the relationship between North America and Europe.
vocabulary.example: Few cities are more emblematic of the postwar trans-Atlantic alliance than the German capital.
vocabulary.example_cn: 很少有城市比德国首都更能象征战后跨大西洋联盟。

vocabulary.meaning_en: Unity or agreement of feeling or action, especially among individuals with a common interest; mutual support within a group.
vocabulary.example: In 1963, John F. Kennedy delivered his famous “Ich bin ein Berliner” speech here promising American solidarity during the Soviet blockade.
vocabulary.example_cn: 1963年，约翰·F·肯尼迪在这里发表了他著名的“我是一个柏林人”演讲，承诺在苏联封锁期间美国将与柏林团结一致。

vocabulary.meaning_en: An act or means of sealing off a place to prevent goods or people from entering or leaving.
vocabulary.example: In 1963, John F. Kennedy delivered his famous “Ich bin ein Berliner” speech here promising American solidarity during the Soviet blockade.
vocabulary.example_cn: 1963年，约翰·F·肯尼迪在这里发表了他著名的“我是一个柏林人”演讲，承诺在苏联封锁期间美国将与柏林团结一致。

vocabulary.meaning_en: Be a sign that (something) is about to happen. (In this context: announce or proclaim)
vocabulary.example: And 21 years later, 200,000 Berliners turned out to hear then-Sen. Barack Obama herald a new era of U.S.-European friendship.
vocabulary.example_cn: 21年后，20万柏林人涌上街头，聆听时任参议员巴拉克·奥巴马预示美欧友谊新时代的到来。

vocabulary.meaning_en: Making a loud, confused noise; uproarious. (In this context: characterized by disorder or confusion)
vocabulary.example: After the tumultuous first year of Donald Trump’s second term, few Germans have positive things to say about the current U.S. president, partly because he doesn’t have nice things to say either.
vocabulary.example_cn: 在唐纳德·特朗普第二任期动荡的第一年之后，很少有德国人对现任美国总统有好话，部分原因是他自己也没有好话可说。

vocabulary.meaning_en: Provoke or challenge (someone) with insulting remarks.
vocabulary.example: During a speech in neighboring Switzerland last week, Trump taunted Europeans that without U.S. help in World War II, “you’d all be speaking German.”
vocabulary.example_cn: 上周在邻国瑞士的一次演讲中，特朗普嘲讽欧洲人，称如果没有美国在二战中的帮助，“你们都会说德语”。

vocabulary.example: “ I find the image of the U.S. that Trump is spreading around the world to be absolutely devastating.”
vocabulary.example_cn: “我认为特朗普在世界各地散布的美国形象绝对是毁灭性的。”

vocabulary.meaning_en: One of two or more available possibilities.
vocabulary.example: Welcome to a world where America First is coming to mean America Alone, whose friends are searching for alternatives to what increasingly feels like an abusive relationship and whose enemies are gloating.
vocabulary.example_cn: 欢迎来到一个“美国优先”正逐渐演变为“美国孤立”的世界，在这个世界里，美国的朋友们正在寻找替代方案，以摆脱这种日益感觉像虐待的关系，而美国的敌人则幸灾乐祸。

vocabulary.meaning_en: A relationship in which one person is treated badly or cruelly by another.
vocabulary.example: Welcome to a world where America First is coming to mean America Alone, whose friends are searching for alternatives to what increasingly feels like an abusive relationship and whose enemies are gloating.
vocabulary.example_cn: 欢迎来到一个“美国优先”正逐渐演变为“美国孤立”的世界，在这个世界里，美国的朋友们正在寻找替代方案，以摆脱这种日益感觉像虐待的关系，而美国的敌人则幸灾乐祸。
